title: Design static translator
description:
## Problem
Translation between DSL and NL should be as static/fast as possible, with local model only as optional polish at human boundaries.

## Requirements
- Static rules for NL to DSL (main agent input)
- Static rules for DSL to NL (main agent output to human)
- Template-based expansion for known schemas
- Identify what cannot be done statically
- Caching strategy for common expansions

## Deliverables
- Static translator architecture
- Rule/template format
- Cache design
- Fallback to local model: when and how
acceptance_criteria: Must satisfy validation testcase in docs/validation-testcase.md

title: Implement DSL serializer (structured data → wire form)
description:
## Goal
Implement the DSL serializer that converts structured data back to wire-form DSL.

## Requirements
- Serialize dict/tree to bracket-tag wire form
- Alphabetical attribute ordering
- Single-line output (no pretty-print)
- Deterministic output
- See docs/design/message-schema.md section 6

## Deliverable
src/dsl_serializer.py with serialize_dsl(data: dict) -> str

title: Level 3: Full pipeline with real LLM orchestration
description:
## Goal
Run the full multi-agent pipeline end-to-end with real LLM calls.
Main agent (LLM) receives NL input, decomposes into DSL tasks,
dispatches to subagents (also LLM), collects DSL results,
aggregates into NL for user.

## Architecture
User NL -> Main Agent (LLM) -> DSL task -> Subagent (LLM) -> DSL result
                  ^                                           |
                  |___________ aggregate -> NL _______________|

## Components Needed
1. Main Agent prompt (NL→DSL decomposition + aggregation)
2. Subagent prompts (DSL task → DSL result, per agent type)
3. Pipeline runner that orchestrates the LLM calls
4. Reference resolution (context-ref → actual content)
5. Token counting across all calls

## Validation Criteria
- Main agent produces valid DSL task messages
- Subagents produce valid DSL result messages
- Parser handles all real LLM output
- NL output is coherent and complete
- Token count is measured end-to-end

title: Design task/subagent output generation for humans
description:
## Problem
Humans no longer read the actual code — they read the main agent's summary of what subagents did. This output generation step needs its own design for clarity and minimalism.

## Requirements
- DSL-to-NL expansion at the main agent boundary
- Output templates per agent type (reviewer output, tester output, coder output)
- Minimal but informative — avoid re-describing what the user already knows
- Highlight what changed, what was decided, what needs attention
- Support different verbosity levels (summary vs detailed)

## Deliverables
- Output generation template format
- Per-agent-type output templates
- Verbosity level design
- NL rendering rules from structured DSL
acceptance_criteria: Must satisfy validation testcase in docs/validation-testcase.md

title: Design agent process definition format
description:
## Problem
The agent topology, roles, schemas, and message types need a declarative definition that can be loaded at runtime.

## Requirements
- Declare agent types and their roles
- Associate input/output schemas per agent type
- Define the process DAG (which agents communicate with which)
- Schema registry — where schemas live, how agents load them
- Support for process composition (pipelines, loops, fan-out/fan-in)

## Deliverables
- Process definition YAML format
- Schema registry design
- Process composition primitives (pipeline, parallel, loop, conditional)
acceptance_criteria: Must satisfy validation testcase in docs/validation-testcase.md
notes: Design complete. See docs/design/agent-process.md. Follow-up task llm-dsl-fao filed for composition model improvements.

title: Design schema drift and unknown field handling
description:
## Problem
When subagent A emits fields that subagent B's schema doesn't understand, the system must not break. Need a passthrough mechanism.

## Requirements
- Unknown fields must be preserved, not dropped
- Forward compatibility — newer schemas don't break older consumers
- Schema versioning strategy
- Validation: strict vs permissive modes

## Deliverables
- Unknown field passthrough mechanism
- Schema versioning approach
- Validation mode definitions
acceptance_criteria: Must satisfy validation testcase in docs/validation-testcase.md

title: Design message schema format
description:
## Problem
Subagents need a structured, composable message format that is compact yet unambiguous.

## Requirements
- Composable (tasks nest, subtasks reference each other)
- Schema-first but extensible per agent type
- Support references (task IDs, message IDs, context references) instead of re-sending data
- Lightweight type system for unambiguity
- Deterministic output for same semantic content

## Deliverables
- Schema definition format (YAML)
- Core message schema (task, result, context, file, fn, error, test)
- Type system definition
- Reference mechanism design
acceptance_criteria: Must satisfy validation testcase in docs/validation-testcase.md

title: Implement process definition loader and validator
description:
## Goal
Implement loading and validation of process.yaml files.

## Requirements
- Parse YAML process definition
- Validate schema definitions
- Validate topology (routes, agent references)
- Validate composition (no cycles, all refs resolve)
- Build schema registry
- See docs/design/agent-process.md

## Deliverable
src/process.py with load_process(path: str) -> ProcessDefinition

title: Implement validation testcase runner
description:
## Goal
Implement the end-to-end validation testcase from docs/validation-testcase.md.

## Requirements
- Simulate the full 7-message flow
- Measure token counts (using tiktoken cl100k_base)
- Compare NL baseline vs DSL
- Validate all 7 acceptance criteria
- Output a report

## Deliverable
tests/test_validation.py with full testcase + token measurement report

title: Implement DSL parser (wire form → structured data)
description:
## Goal
Implement the DSL parser that converts wire-form DSL into structured data (dict/tree).

## Requirements
- Parse bracket-tag form: [tag k=v]content[/tag]
- Handle nested tags
- Handle text content
- Handle passthrough/unknown tags
- Deterministic output
- See docs/design/message-schema.md for grammar

## Deliverable
src/dsl_parser.py with parse_dsl(wire_form: str) -> dict

title: Revisit composition model — improve beyond nested pipeline/parallel
description:
## Problem
The current composition model (§4.4 in agent-process.md) uses nested pipeline/parallel primitives. This works but has gaps:

- Expressing arbitrary DAGs is awkward with nesting
- Conditional branching and loops don't compose cleanly with parallel
- The 'virtual step' pattern (aggregate, evaluate) is a hack
- No clear way to express 'wait for any 2 of 3' or 'first-success' semantics
- No way to express agent-to-agent data dependencies beyond context-from

## How to Improve

Needs more research and design. Ideas to explore:
- DAG-based composition with explicit dependency edges (not nesting)
- First-class aggregation / reduce step as a primitive (not virtual)
- Fan-in semantics: wait-for-all, wait-for-any, wait-for-k-of-n
- Explicit data flow between steps (not just implicit context-ref)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Must express all patterns in the validation testcase
- Must support: pipeline, parallel, conditional, loop, DAG
- Must be at least as readable as nested composition
- Must not add significant token cost to process definitions

title: Implement agent harness (orchestrator + subagent simulation)
description:
## Goal
Implement the agent harness that simulates the multi-agent workflow.

## Requirements
- Main agent: receives NL, decomposes into tasks, dispatches to subagents
- Subagent simulation: receives DSL task, produces DSL result
- Sequential simulation (no real concurrency)
- Error handling with retry policy
- Context budget tracking
- See docs/design/communication-protocol.md

## Deliverable
src/harness.py with run_process(process: ProcessDefinition, user_input: str) -> str

title: Implement static translator (DSL → NL)
description:
## Goal
Implement the static DSL-to-NL translator using expansion rules and templates.

## Requirements
- Expand known DSL tags to NL fragments using static rules
- Fill per-agent-type output templates
- Handle passthrough tags generically
- No LLM calls — pure Python
- See docs/design/static-translator.md and docs/design/human-output.md

## Deliverable
src/translator.py with dsl_to_nl(wire_form: str, agent_type: str) -> str

title: Design bootstrapping and schema distribution
description:
## Problem
Every subagent needs the schema in its system prompt. For small/simple tasks, this overhead might negate DSL token savings.

## Requirements
- Minimize schema overhead in system prompts
- Lazy schema loading — only load what an agent type needs
- Measure and quantify the break-even point (when does DSL save tokens vs. raw NL)
- Schema caching across invocations

## Deliverables
- Schema distribution strategy
- Overhead measurement approach
- Break-even analysis framework
acceptance_criteria: Must satisfy validation testcase in docs/validation-testcase.md

title: Define validation testcase for PoC
description:
## Problem
Before building the PoC pipeline, we need a concrete testcase that validates the entire flow end-to-end. This testcase serves as our acceptance criteria.

## Requirements
- Realistic multi-agent workflow scenario
- Covers: main agent orchestration, subagent dispatch, DSL inter-agent communication, aggregation, NL output to human
- Measurable: token count comparison (NL vs DSL)
- Exercises all 7 design areas

## Scenario
TBD — to be designed

## Deliverables
- Full scenario description
- Expected message flow (sequence diagram)
- Expected token savings measurement methodology
- Acceptance criteria

title: Design communication protocol
description:
## Problem
Agents need a well-defined protocol for dispatching tasks, collecting results, propagating errors, and managing the agent lifecycle.

## Requirements
- Task dispatch from main agent to subagents
- Result collection and aggregation
- Error propagation with structured error codes
- Retry / reassign / escalate decisions
- Parallel subagent execution and result merging
- Context window management (accumulated DSL summarization)

## Deliverables
- Protocol state machine
- Message flow diagrams
- Error handling taxonomy
- Context accumulation and summarization strategy
acceptance_criteria: Must satisfy validation testcase in docs/validation-testcase.md

title: Define validation strategy for PoC
description:
## Problem
We have a working PoC with 7 passing acceptance criteria, but these are
self-contained tests with hardcoded subagent responses. We need to validate
that the DSL actually works with real LLM output.

## Key Risks to Validate

1. **LLM compliance**: Will real LLMs actually produce valid DSL output when
   instructed to do so? Or will they ignore the format and produce prose?

2. **Token savings with real output**: Our NL baseline was hand-written.
   Real LLMs produce different verbosity. Need to measure with actual output.

3. **Parser robustness**: Our parser handles the test cases. Will it handle
   the malformed output a real LLM might produce?

4. **Translation quality**: Does the static DSL→NL expansion produce
   natural-sounding output for real DSL input?

5. **Schema drift in practice**: Do real subagents produce unexpected fields?

6. **Multi-turn workflows**: Does the DSL hold up across iterative
   review → fix → re-review cycles?

## Validation Approaches

### Level 1: Unit Testing (current state)
- Parser round-trip tests
- Serializer determinism tests
- Template expansion tests

### Level 2: Integration Testing (next step)
- Run real LLM calls with DSL-instructed prompts
- Measure actual token counts
- Validate parser handles real LLM output

### Level 3: Comparative Benchmarking
- Run the same task with and without DSL
- Compare token usage, latency, output quality
- Measure at different workflow sizes (1 hop, 3 hops, 10 hops)

### Level 4: Edge Case Testing
- Malformed DSL from LLM
- Very large context windows
- Schema drift scenarios
- Error/retry propagation

## Deliverables
- validation/ directory with test suites at each level
- Benchmark scripts for token measurement
-报告和报告
notes: Level 2 validation complete. 5/5 LLM compliance tests pass with pi CLI. See validation/llm_compliance_test.py and validation/results.json
